name: Support Team
description: A multiagent customer support organization with ticket triage, SLA monitoring, escalation handling, and knowledge base maintenance. Handles high-volume support with automated prioritization.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Support Lead
agents.role: Support manager — monitors SLA compliance, handles escalation decisions, coaches agents, and reports on CSAT metrics

agents.name: Support Agent
agents.role: Frontline support — responds to tickets, troubleshoots issues, documents solutions, and maintains first-response SLA

agents.name: Escalation Engineer
agents.role: Senior technical support — handles complex escalations, debugs difficult issues, and creates permanent fixes

agents.name: Knowledge Base Manager
agents.role: Documentation — maintains FAQ, writes help articles, identifies documentation gaps from ticket patterns, and trains agents

communities.name: Support Team
communities.description: All support team coordination and announcements

groups.name: Escalations
groups.description: Escalated issues requiring senior engineering attention

groups.name: Knowledge Base
groups.description: KB article creation, updates, and gap analysis

workflows.name: Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the support team and set SLA targets
workflows.content:
# Support Team Kickoff

You are the Support Lead. Your team just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Product/service:** [e.g., ClawMax multiagent platform]
- **SLA targets:** [first response <1hr + resolution <24hr, first response <30min + resolution <4hr, first response <15min + resolution <1hr, custom]
- **Ticket source:** [GitHub issues, email, Zendesk, Jira, Intercom, custom]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — for tracking issues]
- **Escalation criteria:** [P0=outage P1=broken-feature P2=degraded P3=cosmetic, P0=critical P1=high P2=medium P3=low, custom]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Support Team community
2. Review the workspace for existing tickets or customer data
3. Set SLA targets and escalation policies
4. Assign initial ticket queues to support agents
5. Brief the knowledge manager on priority documentation areas

workflows.name: Ticket Triage
workflows.description: Triage and prioritize incoming support tickets
workflows.content:
# Ticket Triage Cycle

1. Pull all unassigned tickets since last triage
2. Categorize by priority (P0 = outage, P1 = broken feature, P2 = question, P3 = enhancement)
3. P0/P1: immediately assign to escalation engineer and notify support lead
4. P2/P3: assign to support agents based on current load and expertise
5. Update ticket status and post triage summary to Triage group

workflows.name: SLA Monitoring
workflows.description: Monitor SLA compliance and flag at-risk tickets
workflows.content:
# SLA Monitoring

1. Check all open tickets against SLA thresholds
2. Flag tickets approaching SLA breach (80% of allowed time)
3. For breached SLAs: notify support lead and escalate if needed
4. Calculate current SLA compliance rate and CSAT score
5. Post hourly SLA dashboard to Status group

workflows.name: Knowledge Base Updates
workflows.description: Weekly KB gap analysis and article updates
workflows.content:
# Weekly KB Review

1. Analyze resolved tickets from the past week — identify recurring questions
2. Check if existing KB articles cover the top 10 ticket topics
3. Draft new articles for any gaps found
4. Update outdated articles based on product changes
5. Post KB update summary and link new articles in Knowledge Base group
